title: Chemical Engineer (Material Planning) #75663
description:
Job Description

  • Industry/ Organization Type: Waste Management
  • Position Title: Chemical Engineer (Material Planning)
  • Working Location: Tuas (Company transport provided)
  • Working Hours: 5 days (Mon - Fri, 8.00am - 5.00pm)
  • Salary Package: Basic salary up to $3,800 + AWS + Variable Bonus
  • Duration: Permanent Role

Key Responsibilities

  • Supervise and manage repacking, containment, ICW operations, and maintenance activities
  • Monitor the safe process flow and backlog levels of TIW waste
  • Ensure staff are trained according to the On-the-Job Training program and follow safe work procedures and SOPs
  • Coordinate with operations, sales, and customer service teams to maintain high service quality
  • Track containment purchase budgets and promote recycling of used containment to reduce costs
  • Perform daily inspections of operations, check plant 5S and TPM activities, and monitor safety, equipment, and machinery
  • Lead daily safety toolbox meetings and ensure personal protective equipment (PPE) is adequate for all staff
  • Review existing procedures and workflows to improve efficiency and throughput
  • Implement continuous improvement and modification plans for optimal operations
  • Collaborate with lab chemists to evaluate and develop new treatment methods and procedures for new or unknown wastes, and report on operation costs
  • Ensure compliance with EHS standards and support continual improvement of ISO 45001
  • Prepare and analyze daily and monthly operational records and reports, address any issues, and follow up with relevant departments
  • Compile annual operation and maintenance budgets for approval and monitor expenditures
  • Work with operations and engineering teams to develop and implement preventive maintenance schedules
  • Conduct regular training for staff on safe waste handling, MSDS, PPE, and risk assessment
  • Activate the emergency response team and respond to spills or fire incidents as needed; work with the EHS department to ensure proper emergency preparedness and conduct regular drills

APPLY NOW!!!

  • Degree in Chemical Engineering or related discipline
  • 2 years of relevant experience

Kindly apply through ANY of the following methods:

  • Submit your application by clicking the APPLY button;
  • Email your resume to Job@anradus.com.sg. Please indicate #75663 on the email subject.

Anradus Application Policy: We value each application and ensure every resume is reviewed. Our process is efficient, typically concluding within 3 working days. If you do not receive communication from us within this timeframe, it's likely that your application has not been shortlisted by our client. In such cases, we recommend continuing your job search to maximize your opportunities.
